title: Piper ceramicum
intro: Piper ceramicum, also known as Long Pepper, is a flowering plant in the pepper family (Piperaceae) native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and prevent infections. The bioactive constituents of Piper ceramicum include piperine, a bioavailability enhancer, as well as other alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Piper ceramicum is commonly prepared as a powder, decoction, or oil, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as to enhance digestion and circulation.
taxonomy: Piper ceramicum is classified within the family Piperaceae and is a species of flowering plant in the genus Piper. This genus contains over 1,000 species of plants, commonly known as pepper plants or pipe vines. The family Piperaceae is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. The plants in this family are known for their aromatic leaves and are often used in traditional medicine. Piper ceramicum is native to Central and South America.

Its leaves and roots are known to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating wounds and reducing pain. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia.
